subject: A pair of elf assassins crouched in a forest clearing, each wielding a curved blade, poised mid-stalk
description: Two long-eared elven figures crouch low in a dim forest, each gripping a single curved blade. The pair are mirrored — left and right hands of a single killer-unit — both clad in muted leather and cloth in earthy greens and browns, with hooded or wrap-bound heads. Their poses are tense and feline: weight low, knees bent, blades held out and back in a striking stance. The forest behind them is rendered in deep blue-green shadow with shafts of pale light cutting through the canopy, suggesting twilight or pre-dawn. The composition is symmetrical, predatory, and quiet — a moment held just before violence.
foreground: two crouched elf assassins with drawn curved blades
background: shadowed forest with distant pale light filtering through trees
emotion: focused, predatory, silent intent — bodies coiled to strike
